issue.author: shao-hua-li

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body:
Bisected to `DSEPass`.
Reduced test case: https://godbolt.org/z/ah3jj474s

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body:
Further reduced using Alive2:

```llvm
define i8 @test(i64 %off) {
entry:
  %p = alloca [2 x i8], align 1
  %ext = zext i1 false to i64
  %add625 = add nuw nsw i64 %off, 1
  %add726 = add nuw nsw i64 %add625, %ext
  %sext27 = shl i64 %add726, 32
  %arrayidx29 = getelementptr inbounds [2 x i8], ptr %p, i64 0, i64 %sext27
  store i8 0, ptr %arrayidx29, align 1
  %l = load i8, ptr %p, align 1
  ret i8 %l
}
```

BasicAA determines `%arrayidx29` does not alias `%p`, but it can, if the `shl` shifts out all non-zero bits. Probably we are not accounting properly for wrapping. Looking

issue.title: Wrong code at -O2 on x86_64-linux_gnu(recent regression)

patch:
commit 2d39cb49833abaf7a67110149a010940d7790d7e
Author: Florian Hahn <flo@fhahn.com>
Date:   Tue Nov 21 14:27:50 2023 +0000

    [BasicAA] Don't use MinAbsVarIndex = 1. (#72993)
    
    The current code incorrectly assumed that the absolute variable index
    needs to be at least 1, if the variable is != 0. This is incorrect, in
    case multiplying with Scale wraps.
    
    The code below already checks for wrapping properly, so just remove the
    incorrect assignment.
    
    Fixes https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/72831.

diff --git a/llvm/lib/Analysis/BasicAliasAnalysis.cpp b/llvm/lib/Analysis/BasicAliasAnalysis.cpp
index bf766ee67bb3..4d2f81ea4cfc 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Analysis/BasicAliasAnalysis.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Analysis/BasicAliasAnalysis.cpp
@@ -1230,9 +1230,6 @@ AliasResult BasicAAResult::aliasGEP(
     const VariableGEPIndex &Var = DecompGEP1.VarIndices[0];
     if (Var.Val.TruncBits == 0 &&
         isKnownNonZero(Var.Val.V, DL, 0, &AC, Var.CxtI, DT)) {
-      // If V != 0, then abs(VarIndex) > 0.
-      MinAbsVarIndex = APInt(Var.Scale.getBitWidth(), 1);
-
       // Check if abs(V*Scale) >= abs(Scale) holds in the presence of
       // potentially wrapping math.
       auto MultiplyByScaleNoWrap = [](const VariableGEPIndex &Var) {

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "p:64:64:64"

define i8 @mul_nsw_var_nonzero_minabsvarindex_one_index(ptr %arr, i8 %x, i64 %v) {
  %1 = load [2000 x i8], ptr %arr, align 1
  %or = or i64 %v, 1
  %idx = mul nsw i64 %or, 1844674407370955
  %gep.idx = getelementptr inbounds [2000 x i8], ptr %arr, i32 0, i64 %idx
  %l = load i8, ptr %gep.idx, align 1
  %gep.917 = getelementptr inbounds [2000 x i8], ptr %arr, i32 0, i32 917
  store i8 0, ptr %gep.917, align 1
  %gep.0 = getelementptr inbounds [2000 x i8], ptr %arr, i32 0, i32 0
  store i8 0, ptr %gep.0, align 1
  ret i8 %l
}

target datalayout = "p:64:64:64"

define i8 @test_pr72831_may_wrap(i64 %off) {
entry:
  %p = alloca [2 x i8], align 1
  %ext = zext i1 false to i64
  %add.1 = add nuw nsw i64 %off, 1
  %add.2 = add nuw nsw i64 %add.1, %ext
  %idx = shl i64 %add.2, 32
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds [2 x i8], ptr %p, i64 0, i64 %idx
  store i8 0, ptr %gep, align 1
  %l = load i8, ptr %p, align 1
  ret i8 %l
}

target datalayout = "p:64:64:64"

define i8 @mul_may_overflow_var_nonzero_minabsvarindex_one_index(ptr %arr, i8 %x, i64 %v) {
  %1 = load [2000 x i8], ptr %arr, align 1
  %or = or i64 %v, 1
  %idx = mul i64 %or, 1844674407370955
  %gep.idx = getelementptr inbounds [2000 x i8], ptr %arr, i32 0, i64 %idx
  %l = load i8, ptr %gep.idx, align 1
  %gep.917 = getelementptr inbounds [2000 x i8], ptr %arr, i32 0, i32 917
  store i8 0, ptr %gep.917, align 1
  %gep.0 = getelementptr inbounds [2000 x i8], ptr %arr, i32 0, i32 0
  store i8 0, ptr %gep.0, align 1
  ret i8 %l
}
